config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan atas nama “kepentingan masyarakat” kalau tidak ada batas yang jelas, karena itu mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat masyarakat Indonesia, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi martabat dan hak dasar tiap orang. Jadi, yang benar bukan mendahulukan satu pihak secara mutlak, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse of power. Individual rights are the whole point of constitutional limits here: free speech, due process, privacy, and equal protection are supposed to protect people precisely when the majority wants to override them. That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, because public safety and basic order sometimes require limits, but those limits need to be narrow, justified, and accountable. If “society” becomes a vague excuse to silence dissent or treat minorities as expendable, then it is no longer protecting the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara otomatis. Di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan itu berbahaya karena bisa menutup kritik, membungkam minoritas, atau melegalkan penyalahgunaan wewenang. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlakuan adil, dan martabat manusia. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar adalah keseimbangan yang ketat: masyarakat dilindungi, tapi individu tidak boleh dijadikan korban atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, whenever people say “the common good” without strong limits, it has often ended up justifying censorship, discrimination, or government overreach against unpopular groups. Public safety and social order matter, but they should be used to justify only narrow, accountable restrictions, not a blank check to override basic rights. If society is healthy, it protects individuals rather than asking them to sacrifice their rights whenever the majority finds it convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat situasi di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai pembenaran untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau memberi ruang bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang berani membatasi diri agar hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlakuan adil, dan martabat manusia tetap terlindungi. Jadi menurut saya, yang masuk akal bukan prioritas mutlak untuk salah satu pihak, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dan adil dengan batas yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should generally outrank the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any vague appeal to “the common good,” because it has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or government abuse against unpopular people. That does not mean individual rights are limitless, but any restriction should be narrow, clearly justified, and subject to real accountability. If society is asking people to give up basic rights whenever it is convenient, then it is not serving the public well at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
